growth_records.medium: DSMZ medium 1718 (physiology/growth-rate determination); routine cultivation medium II
growth_records.cite: Pradel, Nathalie et al. Description and genome analysis of a novel archaeon isolated from a syntrophic pyrite-forming enrichment culture and reclassification of Methanospirillum hungatei strains GP1 and SK as Methanospirillum purgamenti sp. nov.. PLOS ONE (2024). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0308405. [PMC11346949]
growth_records.snippet: Under optimal conditions, a maximum growth rate of 0.03 h-1 was obtained. The corresponding generation time of 10 hours ... The generation time at 37C is 10.0-13.0 h. Strictly anaerobic metabolism with CO2 as electron acceptor, which is red
growth_records.notes: Type-strain description paper. Measured (OD600) maximum specific growth rate mu = 0.03 h-1 under optimal conditions (35-37C, pH 7.0-7.2, 3 g/L NaCl). NOTE internal inconsistency in the paper: mu=0.03 h-1 implies td=ln2/0.03=23.1 h, yet the paper states a generation/doubling time of 10.0-13.0 h (which would imply mu=0.052-0.069 h-1); both figures are reported verbatim, doubling_time recorded as the paper's stated 10.0-13.0 h. Metabolism: hydrogenotrophic/formatotrophic methanogenesis, CO2 -> CH4, with H2, formate or 2-propanol as electron donors and acetate obligately required as carbon source. Methane production was measured qualitatively by GC-FID but NO quantitative uptake (H2/formate/CO2/acetate) or secretion (CH4) rate is reported, so uptake_rates/secretion_rates left empty. Growth-rate assays done in DSMZ medium 1718 (composition referenced to DSMZ, not restated); the fully stated defined medium II composition is listed here (per liter distilled water) as the closest documented routine medium. N2 fixation possible (nitrogenase present).
